input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's your complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS:

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [https://dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download)
- Install the latest **.NET 8 SDK** (or .NET 9 if you want the newest). MAUI ships as a workload inside the SDK.
- Discover versions: the download page always shows current/LTS releases.
- Verify: `dotnet --version`

## 2. Install Xcode (for iOS)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [https://developer.apple.com/xcode/](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install the **latest stable Xcode** (requires an Apple ID).
- After install, run:
  ```
  sudo xcode-select --switch /Applications/Xcode.app
  xcodebuild -license accept
  ```
- Install Command Line Tools: `xcode-select --install`
- Verify: `xcodebuild -version`

## 3. Install Android SDK (for Android)

Two options — pick one:

**Option A — Android Studio (recommended):**
- **Source:** [https://developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio)
- Open Android Studio → SDK Manager → install latest Android SDK, Build-Tools, and Command-line Tools.
- Note the SDK path (usually `~/Library/Android/sdk`).

**Option B — Command-line only:**
- Install via the .NET MAUI workload (step 4), which can auto-provision the Android SDK.

## 4. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
dotnet workload install maui
```

- This pulls in `maui-android`, `maui-ios`, `maui-maccatalyst`, and all dependencies.
- Discover available workloads: `dotnet workload search`
- Verify installed workloads: `dotnet workload list`

## 5. Set Environment Variables

Add to your `~/.zshrc` (or `~/.bash_profile`):

```bash
export ANDROID_HOME="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk"
export PATH="$PATH:$ANDROID_HOME/platform-tools:$ANDROID_HOME/tools"
export JAVA_HOME="/Applications/Android Studio.app/Contents/jbr/Contents/Home"
```

> The MAUI workload can auto-manage `JAVA_HOME` — only set it manually if builds can't find Java.

## 6. Accept Android Licenses

```bash
$ANDROID_HOME/cmdline-tools/latest/bin/sdkmanager --licenses
```

## 7. Verify the Full Setup

```bash
dotnet workload list            # should show "maui" installed
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui    # scaffold a project
cd HelloMaui

# iOS (requires Xcode simulator):
dotnet build -f net8.0-ios      # or net9.0-ios

# Android:
dotnet build -f net8.0-android  # or net9.0-android
```

Run the **MAUI Check tool** for a one-shot diagnostic:

```bash
dotnet tool install -g Redth.Net.Maui.Check
maui-check
```

This scans for missing SDKs, wrong versions, and misconfigured paths — and offers to fix them.

## Version Discovery Cheat Sheet

| Component | How to find latest version |
|---|---|
| .NET SDK | `dotnet --list-sdks` or [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload search maui` |
| Xcode | App Store or `xcodebuild -version` |
| Android SDK | Android Studio SDK Manager or `sdkmanager --list` |

## Install Order

**Xcode → .NET SDK → `dotnet workload install maui` → Android Studio (optional) → accept licenses → `maui-check`**

Xcode goes first because it's the largest download and the MAUI workload expects its command-line tools to already be present.
